The steppes of Southern Russia exhibit similar flat terrain and agricultural use, though the utility pole infrastructure and road maintenance typically differ from the North American style seen here.
The vast open plains and soil composition are similar to the Kazakh steppe, although the specific vegetation and road layout are more reminiscent of North American prairies.
